# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create AWS CloudFormation template for SQS queue and SNS topic setup. Define queue with message retention and visibility timeout. Set up SNS topic for pub/sub messaging. Configure queue policy to allow message operations. This is for our serverless event processing architecture.
# Category: sqs_policy_too_permissive

Resources: 
    MyQueueName: ## AWS CloudFormation ParameterStore (or you can use SSM parameter store) or Secrets Manager with name "MyAmazonSQSServiceRole" for RDS credentials if not set manually. Default is AmazonSQSFullAccess - replace this role as per your security requirements
        Type: 'AWS::SQS::Queue'  # This type refers to S Queue in AWS (Simple Queuing Service). The CloudFormation will create a new queue with the specified name and properties if not set. Default settings are messageRetentionPeriodSeconds = 86400, visibilityTimeoutSec = 300
        Properties:         
            QueueName: !Sub ${self:provider.stage}-MyQueue # this creates an unique ID for our SQS resource by appending the stage name to it (optional). This will make every stack's queue have a distinctive id, e.g., dev-myqueue vs prod-otherprod
            RetentionPeriodSeconds: 86400    
    MySNSTopicName: ## AWS CloudFormation ParameterStore or Secrets Manager for the name of SNS topic if not set manually (optional). Default is default_lambda_topic. This will be your publishing event to any subscribers in case you want them subscribed and handling messages later when they are published on that specific queue/topics
        Type: 'AWS::SNS::Topic'  # Creates an SNS topic with the specified name if not set manually or via parameter store. Default is default_lambda_topic (optional). You will be provided a unique ARN by CloudFormation when you create this resource as it can have different values depending on your setup
    MySubscription:   ## AWS Region for subscription, defaults to the region of SQS queue if not set manually.  This is useful so that message subscribers are in sync with each other's publishers and topic subscriptions should be updated regularly as users may add/remove subscriber addresses via notifications
        Type: 'AWS::SNS::Subscription'   # Creates an SNS Subsciption to the provided Topic. The CloudFormation will create a new subscription with its associated ARN, ProtocolARNs and Endpoint values if not set manually or in parameter store (optional).
        Properties:    
            Type: 'SQS'  # This is the protocol that you are using for your SNS topic - Amazon Simple Queue Service. Default value when nothing provided by CF/parametersstore, otherwise it will be overwritten with whatever was set there manually or via parameter store (optional). AWS::SES..SnsTopic
            Topic: !Ref MySNSTopicName   # This is the ARN of our SNS topic that we want to subscribe. Default value when nothing provided by CF/parametersstore, otherwise it will be overwritten with whatever was set there manually or via parameter store (optional).  AWS::SQS..MyQueue
            Endpoint: !Sub ${self:provider.stage}-sqs-queue URL if not configured in CloudFormation then SSM Parameter Store for sqs queue url, Otherwise it will be overwritten with the value from provided manually or via parameter store (optional).  Default is a random AWS generated ARN by default_lambda
            Protocol: 'SQS' # This protocol that you are using to interact in your Lambda function. Default when not configured explicitly - Amazon SQS, otherwise will be overwritten with the value from CF/parametersstore or manually (optional).  Also it can also override this setting as provided by CloudFormation if set directly via parameters store for security reasons
